target_audience: Korean middle-aged and senior viewers, roughly 45-65
viewer_anxiety: I thought I would slow down by now, but I still need income.
Retirement savings, pension, housing, and family costs do not line up.
Supporting adult children and aging parents can overlap.
I do not know whether this is my personal failure or a structural problem.
core_emotion: quiet pressure, fatigue, and a need to be understood without being blamed
content_angle: Explain why many people in their 50s cannot retire as a personal, family, and structural squeeze rather than a simple failure to save.
why_now: This is a draft angle for a Korean longform YouTube pilot. Current retirement, pension, welfare, housing, and labor-market facts still need manual verification before final script writing.
core_conflict: The viewer wants dignity and rest, but income needs, family responsibility, housing costs, and uncertainty about pension/welfare support keep pushing retirement away.
video_promise: By the end, viewers should be able to name the pressures keeping them at work and separate realistic next steps from shame or blame.

key_claims.claim: Many people in their 50s experience retirement as a staged transition rather than a clean stop from work.
source_notes: Verify with current Korean labor participation, retirement age, and reemployment data.
key_claims.overclaim_warning: Do not claim a national average or trend without current official data.

key_claims.claim: Retirement pressure is often created by several costs arriving at once: housing, children, parents, health, and income replacement.
source_notes: Use as a story structure until backed by household finance and welfare data.
key_claims.overclaim_warning: Do not imply every household has the same burden.

key_claims.claim: Pension and welfare expectations should be handled carefully because rules and benefit amounts change.
source_notes: Verify pension, welfare, and eligibility facts against current official sources before recording.
key_claims.overclaim_warning: Do not give pension planning advice or imply guaranteed benefits.

key_claims.claim: The strongest video angle is not panic, but recognition: many viewers need language for why retirement feels delayed.
source_notes: This is a content angle, not a verified factual claim.
key_claims.overclaim_warning: Do not present audience psychology as survey data.

notes: This pilot packet is a draft content-production example, not a completed evidence run.
No verified citations are included in this example.
Before final script writing, verify labor, pension, welfare, housing, and household finance claims.

title_candidates.title: 50대가 은퇴를 못 하는 진짜 이유
title_candidates.note: Clear main title; avoids unsupported numeric claims.

title_candidates.title: 쉰 살이 넘었는데 왜 아직도 일을 놓지 못할까
은퇴가 늦어지는 건 게을러서가 아닙니다
title_candidates.note: Needs careful evidence support to avoid overgeneralization.

hook_candidates.hook: 분명 젊을 때는 쉰 넘으면 조금 쉬어도 될 줄 알았습니다. 그런데 현실은 반대인 분들이 많습니다.
은퇴를 못 하는 이유를 저축 부족 하나로만 보면, 정작 중요한 압박을 놓치게 됩니다.
hook_candidates.note: Must be backed by verified examples later.

beats: Address viewers who expected life to ease after 50.
State that the episode is about pressures, not blame.

story_structure.purpose: Frame retirement delay as a layered pressure.
beats: Income does not stop needing to exist just because age changes.
Savings, pension timing, work availability, and family costs collide.

story_structure.purpose: Give realistic scenes that viewers recognize.
beats: Adult child support or wedding/housing help.
Aging parent care or medical costs.
Reemployment that pays less but still becomes necessary.

story_structure.purpose: Separate personal shame from structural pressure.
beats: Explain that retirement readiness depends on income, assets, family burden, health, and policy rules.
Mark all pension/welfare specifics as requiring official verification.

story_structure.purpose: Surface the emotional conflict without blaming generations.
beats: Parents may feel responsible for children.
Children may feel the older generation had more chances.

story_structure.purpose: Give practical but non-advisory next steps.
beats: List what to verify personally: pension timing, fixed costs, family support expectations, health/work capacity.
Encourage preparing questions before making financial decisions.

story_structure.purpose: Close with dignity and a follow-up path.
beats: Restate that delayed retirement is not just a character flaw.
Tease follow-up on what to check before leaving work.

risk_flags.mitigation: Avoid blaming one administration, party, or generation unless supported by verified evidence.
Use general information only; tell viewers to verify their own pension, debt, and income situation.
Check current official pension and welfare sources before final script.
Avoid parent-vs-child blame framing; present overlapping pressures.
Avoid absolute claims such as 'most people cannot retire' unless verified.

source_notes: Need current pension and welfare eligibility data.
Need household finance or debt data.
Need counterevidence: cases where retirement is delayed by choice rather than pressure.
overclaim_warnings: Do not claim that all people in their 50s cannot retire.
Do not give pension or investment advice.
Do not cite pension, welfare, or housing details without current verification.
Do not blame adult children, parents, employers, or government as the sole cause.
Do not present persona reactions as survey evidence.
production_next_actions: Run or import research focused on Korean labor participation, retirement age, pension, welfare, housing, and household finance.
Verify all current pension/welfare details against official sources.
Create Persona Engine TopicCard from this brief and run reaction testing with the money playbook.
Choose 3-5 title candidates for persona testing.
Draft a final script only after risk flags and source notes are resolved.
